title: Remote programming versus standard in-person programming following deep brain stimulation in patients with Parkinson's disease: a randomised controlled trial.
abstract: Remote programming (RP) offers a convenient alternative for patients with Parkinson's disease (PD) who have undergone deep brain stimulation (DBS). However, its clinical efficacy remains underexplored. We aimed to compare the treatment effects of RP and in-person standard programming (SP) in this population.
